prompt: Write me a few LinkedIn posts announcing that our team just shipped a self-serve onboarding flow. Make them sound like a person, not a press release.
reason: Wants feed-native LinkedIn posts, not DMs or lifecycle email.

prompt: I need 3 LinkedIn posts for our founder, one of them a contrarian take about why most onboarding advice is wrong.
reason: Named LinkedIn posts with framings, not paid ads or a brief.

prompt: I have one specific announcement to post on LinkedIn today. Write the post.
reason: Writing a single post, not planning a cadence across properties.

prompt: Draft a Twitter thread breaking down how we cut our render time from 40s to 3s.
reason: Platform-native X thread, keeping the concrete numbers.

prompt: Give me a single punchy tweet with a hot take about AI marketing tools.
reason: One short-form X post, not paid copy or a plan.

prompt: I run 3 product accounts and keep posting to whichever I remember. Help me plan a balanced week of posts across all of them.
reason: Cadence and mix across properties, not writing one post.

prompt: Which of my properties is overdue for a post, and what should the next one be?
reason: Overdue-audit and next-post pick is rotation, not authoring.

prompt: Across my 4 brand accounts, what's the right posting mix for the next 7 days?
reason: 7-day cross-property mix is exactly rotation's job.

prompt: I need a mix of publish-ready content, a few social posts and a short blog intro, that all sound consistently on-brand.
reason: Producing on-voice content, not reviewing or auditing existing copy.

prompt: Write a few on-brand organic social posts and a short blog intro for our sustainability angle.
reason: A voice-matched content set, not paid ads or an SEO brief.

prompt: Write me 5 headline options and a few primary-text variants for a Meta ad promoting our free trial.
reason: Platform paid-ad copy with headline/primary-text structure.

prompt: I need Google Search ad copy variants that fit the character limits for our project-management tool.
reason: Character-limited paid search copy, not an SEO brief.

prompt: Write 5 TikTok ad hooks for our app.
reason: Named-platform ad copy, not organic posts.

prompt: Draft a 4-email welcome sequence for people who just signed up but haven't activated yet.
reason: Lifecycle nurture flow to existing signups.

prompt: We're launching a new plan next month. I need a nurture email flow leading up to it.
reason: Explicitly an email flow, even though a launch is involved.

prompt: Draft the launch-day and follow-up emails for our product launch.
reason: The deliverable is the emails themselves, not the launch plan.

prompt: Write a cold email to a VP of Marketing at a Series B SaaS. Here are three facts about her company. Keep it short.
reason: Cold first-touch from supplied prospect facts.

prompt: I need a LinkedIn connection note and a first DM for prospecting founders at fintech startups.
reason: LinkedIn DMs are outbound, not feed posts.

prompt: I need a cold first-touch email plus one follow-up to a prospect I've never spoken to.
reason: Cold outreach, not a lifecycle nurture sequence.

prompt: One of our customers just told us we saved them 12 hours a week. Turn our call transcript into a case study and some pull quotes.
reason: Case study and quotes from raw customer signal.

prompt: Turn this happy-customer survey response into a one-pager and a few social snippets.
reason: Customer proof turned into reusable formats.

prompt: Build out the main buyer personas for our HR-analytics product, who they are, their pains, what they object to.
reason: Named personas with pains and objections.

prompt: Who are the different types of people who buy dev tools like ours, and what language do they actually use versus how we pitch it?
reason: Personas plus the buyer-vs-vendor language gap.

prompt: Help me figure out who our ideal customer really is, the segments, their pains, their objections.
reason: Segmenting buyers is persona work, not a decision or positioning.

prompt: Give me 4 different versions of our homepage hero headline, each testing a different angle, with a hypothesis for each.
reason: Distinct strategic bets with hypotheses to test.

prompt: I ran two subject-line variants and pasted the open rates below. Which won, and is the sample even big enough to call it?
reason: Adjudicating test results against sample/lift thresholds.

prompt: Give me three strategically different value-prop statements to test against each other, each with what it's betting on.
reason: Variant bets to test, not a single brief or positioning.

prompt: We're doing a Q3 push for our new integration. I need a one-page brief everyone can build against, audience, message, channels.
reason: A single build-against campaign brief.

prompt: Before we make any creative for the back-to-school promo, write the brief: objective, target, single message, hooks.
reason: The brief precedes creative; it is not the creative or a launch plan.

prompt: We're about to launch a new API product and can't agree on what it stands for. Help us nail the one-liner, the wedge, and who it's really for.
reason: Deciding what the launch stands for, not sequencing it.

prompt: What's our positioning against the category, the wedge, the enemy, the proof pillars, for this new feature?
reason: A positioning brief, not a rep battlecard or a 2x2 map.

prompt: We know what we're launching but not what it stands for or how it wins. Help with that, not the timeline.
reason: Positioning explicitly, not the launch timeline.

prompt: Give me a full phased plan for launching next month, what to do pre-launch, launch day, and after, and in what order.
reason: Phased, sequenced launch plan.

prompt: Map out the sequence of assets and channels for our GA launch across the three phases.
reason: Phase-by-phase asset and channel sequencing.

prompt: Give me the phase-by-phase launch sequence. I already have the positioning and the brief.
reason: Only the sequenced playbook is needed now.

prompt: Our reps keep losing deals to Acme. Build me a battlecard, where we win, where we lose, and how to handle their objections.
reason: A rep-facing win/lose/close battlecard against a named competitor.

prompt: Give the sales team a one-page card for competitive deals against Notion, trap questions, objection responses, honest comparison.
reason: Deal-time battlecard, not a market map.

prompt: Plot where we sit versus our three main rivals on a 2x2 and show me the uncontested white space.
reason: A 2x2 positioning map with white-space analysis.

prompt: I want to see how the whole market clusters and where there's open positioning space for us.
reason: Market clustering and white space, not a sales card.

prompt: Here's a draft LinkedIn post. Check it against our brand voice and flag anything off before I publish.
reason: Red-lining one fresh draft before it ships.

prompt: Red-line this landing page copy for banned words and off-tone lines, with rewrites.
reason: A guardrails red-line with suggested rewrites.

prompt: Look at our current homepage and pitch deck and tell me how well they carry our strategy, a scored report with a fix list.
reason: Scored audit of existing published copy against strategy.

prompt: Audit all our existing marketing copy against our messaging pillars and show me where it drifts.
reason: Auditing existing copy for drift, not writing new copy.

prompt: Should we kill our underperforming TikTok channel or give it another quarter? Help me actually decide instead of spiraling.
reason: A triaged go/no-go decision with a revisit date.

prompt: We can't decide whether to rebrand now or after the raise. Walk me to a clear call with a revisit date.
reason: A decision call, not a positioning or audit deliverable.

prompt: I just connected Jinn. Show me what brand data you can actually see and confirm the connection works.
reason: Connection smoke test and DNA read-back.

prompt: Read our Brand DNA back to me so I know what the other skills will ground on.
reason: Orientation read of the connected DNA.

prompt: Turn these 6 bullet points into a self-contained HTML slide deck I can open in a browser.
reason: A single self-contained HTML deck artifact.

prompt: Build me a 1080x1080 Instagram carousel as a single HTML file, on-brand, no build step.
reason: A fixed-crop HTML carousel artifact.

prompt: I need a landing-page hero section as one self-contained HTML file with inline CSS.
reason: An HTML landing section artifact, not positioning copy.

prompt: Here are 10 keywords and our site URL. Build content briefs telling my writers what to cover to rank.
reason: Keyword-driven SEO content briefs.

prompt: What should a blog post about headless CMS migration cover to outrank what's currently ranking? Give me an outline and angle.
reason: Coverage, angle, and outline to rank, not just on-voice copy.
